mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of months following a federal funds rate increase.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and restrictive monetary policy creates stagflationary pressures that erode corporate profit margins and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing demand and healthy supply chain throughput, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of months following a rise in INDPRO during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household consumption capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months following payroll increases exceeding 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1.1 million units signals collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, which precedes broader economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of months where housing starts drop below the 1.1 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income outpacing GDP growth indicates strong household purchasing power and consumption resilience, which fuels corporate top-line growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample predictive regressions over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels crossing above 240 trigger aggressive central bank tightening expectations, which rapidly increases equity discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of trading days following CPIAUCSL readings above 240 during tightening regimes.
